Genuinely the only ‘secure’ login method is 2 factor or token login because they either need access to two of your devices which is unlikely or physical access to a token (or the very unlikely means to cryptographically break the cypher) to get into anything.
doesn't biometrics run into the issue that you like, lose your fingerprint due to losing a finger?
IE, it's not saving your fingerprint, it's creating a key based on the resistance.
You can register more than one, on both hands, but most if not all devices with biometrics let you use a PIN/password, since you don't need to lose a finger for it not to work (wet hands, gloves).
You generally can (and probably should) set up more than one finger as your fingerprint access for your phone or laptop.
If you lose all of them, well... you've probably got bigger problems to worry about.

These hinge tests are fun to watch but most people just want a phone that lasts the whole day.
I have a problem with either of these showing real world conditions.

Both the cover plastic on the OLED screen, plus the OLED technology itself are going to be considerably more damaged by the continual folding and unfolding of the screen in the manner and speed which it is being done.
In a normal real world use situation, the plastics and OLED screen technology would have time to cool, and "heal" between usages.
Many plastics have the ability to heal on a molecular level, reforming or straightening out the polymer chains while at rest, the same for screen technologies.
